features.summary: Meta reported Q1 2022 revenue of $27.9B (up 7% YoY) with net income of $7.5B. Family of Apps revenue grew 6% to $27.2B while Reality Labs revenue increased 30% to $695M but posted a $3B operating loss. The company faces headwinds from Apple's iOS changes, the shift to short-form video (Reels), macro uncertainty from the Ukraine war, and reduced advertising demand. Management lowered 2022 expense guidance to $87-92B from $90-95B previously.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q2 2022 revenue of $28-30B, reflecting continued headwinds from iOS changes, Ukraine war impacts, and the shift to Reels monetization. They anticipate global MAUs to be flat to down sequentially due to Russia restrictions. Long-term, they remain confident in returning to higher revenue growth rates in Family of Apps while funding Reality Labs investments, though acknowledge this won't happen in 2022 given current headwinds.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing Apple iOS privacy changes creating $10B annual headwind, competitive pressure from TikTok in short-form video, macroeconomic uncertainty affecting advertiser demand, regulatory challenges in Europe and globally, and the extended investment cycle for Reality Labs before meaningful monetization. The company also faces potential future platform changes and continued signal loss impacting ad targeting and measurement capabilities.
features.key_drivers: Growth drivers include Reels engagement (20%+ of Instagram time), AI-powered discovery engine expansion, Click-to-Messaging ads showing strong double-digit growth, and Quest 2 VR headset success. The company is investing heavily in AI/ML for ad targeting with less data, developing privacy-enhancing technologies, and building the metaverse platform Horizon with upcoming web version launch and Project Cambria premium headset.
